data.title: Walking Towers
data.statement:
In a tabletop game, towers consisting of tokens move on a triangular grid. There are three types of tokens:

[[P]]: Pikeman. Moves by flipping over one of the sides. Can reach 3 other spaces in a turn.
[[R]]: Rider. Moves by rotating 180 degrees around any vertex. Can reach 3 other spaces in a turn.
[[C]]: Chariot. Moves by rotating 120 degrees around any vertex. Can reach 6 other spaces in a turn.

The Tower can consist of up to 10 tokens. By the rules, the tower moves by making the moves of its tokens, each token can only be used once. So, a tower of two Pikemen can either stay in place, move as a Pikeman or move twice as a Pikeman. The Tower consisting of a Chariot, Rider, and a Pikeman can make up to three moves, each of them different.
data.stubGenerator:
read SIZE:int
read TOWER:string(SIZE)
loop 16 read ROW:string(32)

write OutputMap
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1:>> Number [[N]]: the size of the Tower
<<Line 2:>> String representation of the tower consisting of [[N]] "P", "R" or "C" letters.
<<Next 16 lines:>>  Triangular grid in the format [[ab]] where [[a]] is an upper left triangle of the two-triangle square, and [[b]] is the lower right triangle. The triangle can be either [[_]] (outside of the map), [[0]] (map field), and [[1]] (map field containing the tower).

Only one of the triangles will be equal to 1. That is the tower's position.
data.outputDescription:
16 lines in the same format as an input:
[[__]] if the cell is not used
[[00]], [[01]], [[10]], [[11]] if the upper left or lower right triangle of the cell can be reached by the tower.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

In a tabletop game, towers consisting of tokens move on a triangular grid. There are three types of tokens:

P: Pikeman. Moves by flipping over one of the sides. Can reach 3 other spaces in a turn.
R: Rider. Moves by rotating 180 degrees around any vertex. Can reach 3 other spaces in a turn.
C: Chariot. Moves by rotating 120 degrees around any vertex. Can reach 6 other spaces in a turn.

The Tower can consist of up to 10 tokens. By the rules, the tower moves by making the moves of its tokens, each token can only be used once. So, a tower of two Pikemen can either stay in place, move as a Pikeman or move twice as a Pikeman. The Tower consisting of a Chariot, Rider, and a Pikeman can make up to three moves, each of them different.
Input
Line 1: Number N: the size of the Tower
Line 2: String representation of the tower consisting of N "P", "R" or "C" letters.
Next 16 lines: Triangular grid in the format ab where a is an upper left triangle of the two-triangle square, and b is the lower right triangle. The triangle can be either _ (outside of the map), 0 (map field), and 1 (map field containing the tower).

Only one of the triangles will be equal to 1. That is the tower's position.
Output
16 lines in the same format as an input:
__ if the cell is not used
00, 01, 10, 11 if the upper left or lower right triangle of the cell can be reached by the tower.
Constraints
1 <= N <= 10
Example
Input
1
P
_______________00000000000000000
_____________0000000000000000000
___________000000000000000000000
_________00000000000000000000000
_______0000000000000000000000000
_____000000000000000000000000000
___00000000000000000000000000000
_0000000000000100000000000000000
0000000000000000000000000000000_
00000000000000000000000000000___
000000000000000000000000000_____
0000000000000000000000000_______
00000000000000000000000_________
000000000000000000000___________
0000000000000000000_____________
00000000000000000_______________
Output
_______________00000000000000000
_____________0000000000000000000
___________000000000000000000000
_________00000000000000000000000
_______0000000000000000000000000
_____000000000000000000000000000
___00000000000010000000000000000
_0000000000001110000000000000000
0000000000000000000000000000000_
00000000000000000000000000000___
000000000000000000000000000_____
0000000000000000000000000_______
00000000000000000000000_________
000000000000000000000___________
0000000000000000000_____________
00000000000000000_______________
